### Before submitting an issue, make sure you have:
- [ x] Updated to the latest version
- [x ] Read the [README](https://github.com/instagrambot/instabot/blob/master/README.md) and [docs](https://github.com/instagrambot/instabot/tree/master/docs)
- [x ] [Searched](https://github.com/instagrambot/instabot/search?type=Issues) the bugtracker for similar issues including **closed** ones

### Purpose of your issue?
- [ x] Bug report (encountered problems/errors)
- [ ] Feature request (request for a new functionality)
- [ ] Question
- [ ] Other

---

Code:

```python
in 'ultimate.py'
def stats():
    bot.save_user_stats(bot.username)

schedule.every(1).hours.do(run_threaded, stats)

but this saves the first value over and over. Probably because in get_user_info() there is a check to see if user stats were already asked for. 

def get_user_info(self, user_id):
    user_id = self.convert_to_user_id(user_id)
    if user_id not in self._user_infos:
        self.api.get_username_info(user_id)
        if 'user' not in self.api.last_json:
            return False
        user_info = self.api.last_json['user']
        self._user_infos[user_id] = user_info
    return self._user_infos[user_id]
```

Error/Debug Log:

```python

```


---

### Describe your issue
In the ultimate file we save the usere stats every hour, however bot.save_user_stats() does not save updated stats, but the ones when the function was first run. 
This saves the same value over and over. Probably because in get_user_info() there is a check to see if user stats were already asked for. 

--> add check to see if user stats are requested from own user ID, or remove check altogether and always overwrite when function is called.

### Rationale
For testing and debugging, it would be helpful if `datacube` allowed indexing datasets
with lineage information stripped off. This would help avoid filling test datacubes to hold,
for example, simple statistics on a product with irrelevant lineage data tracing back to
acquisition.

### Expected behaviour
Something along the lines of
`$ datacube dataset add --no-lineage DATASET`
should index dataset with `lineage.source_datasets` set to `{}`.

### Environment information
* Which ``datacube --version`` are you using?
Open Data Cube core, version 1.5.1+516.g7046071
Trent made me do it, I am so sorry.

I do like the elegance of current nursery ```__aexit__``` but wonder if something more explicit would be more pythonic.   My thought was to require awaiting the nursery at the end of the scope (eg ```await nursery.done()```, ```await nursery.run()```, etc) and ```__aexit__``` could just raise an exception if the nursery wasn't closed.  This would also have the advantage of providing something to wrap, allowing users to catch exceptions within the nursery scope (and presumably continue)

Right now we throw some nonsense in the CN field and put the good stuff in the SAN, on the theory that this will help catch any old software that's incorrectly relying on the CN field instead of SAN. But this violates the CABF baseline requirements. We should leave the CN out entirely, which will let us catch broken software while also meeting the requirements.

issue_title: Consider removing emphasis on the `DataFlowKernel` in the docs
issue_body: We might want to relegate discussion of the DFK to the more advanced section of the docs. For many applications, simply running `parsl.load` is sufficient. I strongly feel that as part of the class-based configuration shuffling we're doing, it will be simpler and less bug prone (on our side and user-side) to have the `Config` object be the only mode of specifying options (in other words, dispense with the ability to override options in the DFK constructor-- that removes places people can get confused about what precedence options have. There isn't really a cost; if they are importing a config and then overriding various options, it should be the same if they do it in the `Config` before loading or passing options to the constructor. Overriding options in the `Config` makes it explicit on their side what is going in.) If we _do_ go that route, then exposing the DFK to users seems even less necessary. I'm putting this out there now so I don't forget, but we should make the call after the class-based configs are worked out.

issue_title: Add GraphQL schema auto-generation from the existing database schema

issue_body:
1) Alternative to log or sqrt for "potential_method" - A nice compromise could be the [inverse hyperbolic sine function](http://mathworld.wolfram.com/images/interactive/ArcSinhReal.gif). Continuous from neg. inf. to pos. inf. and exhibits logarithmic properties asymptotically - therefore transforms values without need for imputation. 

Others have begun using this transformation, e.g: [Michael Hoffman](https://twitter.com/michaelhoffman/status/707252800359686144) at U. Toronto. 

2) Mysterious cell in the "remove rare cells" cell - 

`genes_keep=sum(data)>10
data=data(:,genes_keep)
genes=genes(genes_keep)`

3) Alternative method for getting users introduced to method - one way to get people to straight away use the method could be to have the tutorial be housed in a Google CoLab setting, i.e: https://colab.research.google.com/. This would remove any barrier to installation and having an interested person give the code a first pass. Of course, afterwards, they would still need to install it and all that that entails. Distill.pub uses CoLab notebooks to allow interested readers to do exactly this - https://distill.pub/2018/building-blocks/.

issue_title: Support "ANY" HTTP method in API Gateway resources
issue_body:
Hey,

Currently it is not possible to call an API Gateway which has a resource with its HTTP method set to `ANY`. It is however possible to create such a resource in localstack. The  implemented behavior seems to be that localstack actually expects the HTTP method of incoming requests to be `ANY` instead of interpreting `ANY` as "any HTTP methods".

 I quickly investigated and I believe the fix may be done either in [get_apigateway_integration](https://github.com/localstack/localstack/blob/master/localstack/utils/aws/aws_stack.py#L321-L335) or directly in [forward_request](https://github.com/localstack/localstack/blob/master/localstack/services/apigateway/apigateway_listener.py#L157-L169).

Thanks.

issue_title: AttributeError: module 'urllib' has no attribute 'urlencode' (Python 3, repository_blob)
issue_body:
Getting an issue in python 3.6 with the `project.repository_blob` function.
```
File "[blah blah blah]/lib/python3.6/site-packages/gitlab/v3/objects.py", line 1885, in repository_blob
    url += '?%s' % (urllib.urlencode({'filepath': filepath}))
AttributeError: module 'urllib' has no attribute 'urlencode'
```

urlencode was moved in py3 to `urllib.parse.urlencode` (https://docs.python.org/3/library/urllib.parse.html#urllib.parse.urlencode). For maintaining compatibility across python 2 and 3, consider six's [six.moves.urllib](https://pythonhosted.org/six/#module-six.moves) instead of urllib itself.

issue_title: importFile will fail completely if a transient httplib.BadStatusLine error is encountered
issue_body:
I have seen Toil workflows fail with an `httplib.BadStatusLine` error, usually in Jenkins tests, while importing input files. Rerunning the tests fixes the issue; I think the underlying problem is cloud weather causing the remote server to just not send a well-formed HTTP response for that particular connection.

The `importFile` method ought to retry when it encounters a `httplib.BadStatusLine`. I think the answer is to match that type of error here:

https://github.com/DataBiosphere/toil/blob/45de1029ae190b8e0bb3a4addcc7ebc7bec69173/src/toil/lib/retry.py#L139-L140

At https://github.com/fabianp/memory_profiler/blob/master/memory_profiler.py#L66 and https://github.com/fabianp/memory_profiler/blob/master/memory_profiler.py#L70 the line:

mem += getattr(process, meminfo_attr)()[0] / _TWO_20

should be replaced with

mem += getattr(p, meminfo_attr)()[0] / _TWO_20

So the p as the child process iterable in the for loop has its memory captured, instead of just the parent process.

issue_body: 单纯的使每个bar的xaxis设置成不同的数据，结果只能显示最后一个bar的xaxis，前面的均没有作用

<!--
### 提 issue 注意事项
0. 先仔细阅读文档中是否有关于您需要解决的问题的解决方法
1. 提 issue 前请先搜搜看以往的 issue 有没有解决了您的问题
2. 如若是不能正常使用的问题，请贴上详细的 pyecharts 版本，使用系统以及 Python 版本
3. 最好详细描述问题，必要时加上**代码+贴图**
-->

**问题**
鼠标移动到toolbox工具箱上时，提示信息会与工具箱其他图标相互遮掩；
能单独设置提示信息的位置或者直接将提示信息移到图标另一侧吗?


**运行环境（系统环境及 pyecharts 版本）**
linux pyecharts ==0.5.5


**代码及截图**
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/8528552/42307331-9dd478aa-8064-11e8-9dd9-d4925f1bd898.png)

## 问题

目前在渲染一个图表的过程中， `_repr_html__` 总共调用三次 `engine.render` 函数，也就是会创建三个同样的 `EchartsEnvironment` 实例，而 `EchartsEnvironment` 的创建需要花费更多的时间，这里可以合并。

另一方面，同一个 Cell 的模板被分成了 notebook.html / notebook_chart_component.html / notebook_dom.html 等三个文件，可读性较差，可以整合。

## 改进

- 整合三个模板文件，移除 notebook_chart_component.html / notebook_dom.html  等两个文件
- 整合模板变量字典
- 移除 `_render_notebook_component_` 和 `_render_notebook_dom_` 两个方法

## 备注

- 本次改善为内部实现优化，不会改变既有的公有 API
- 该特性预计在 v0.3.4 / v0.4.0 加入

issue_body:
Convert OPTION_ENABLE_TIE_ON OPTION_ENABLE_TIE_OFF to contingency based settings, allowing for other types of tie ons.

CONTINGENCY_TIEON_NONE
CONTINGENCY_TIEON_THREE_SMALL

CONTINGENCY_TIEOFF_NONE
CONTINGENCY_TIEOFF_THREE_SMALL

---

Rename:
CONTINGENCY_NONE = 0xF0
CONTINGENCY_JUMP_NEEDLE = 0xF1
CONTINGENCY_SEW_TO = 0xF2

To:
CONTINGENCY_LONGSTITCH_NONE = 0xF0
CONTINGENCY_LONGSTITCH_JUMP_NEEDLE = 0xF1
CONTINGENCY_LONGSTITCH_SEW_TO = 0xF2

---

Explicit Trim defaults to False.

---

COLOR_CHANGE gets needle selections.
COLOR_CHANGE = 5
NEEDLE_1 = 0x100
NEEDLE_2 = 0x200
NEEDLE_3 = 0x300
NEEDLE_4 = 0x400
...
NEEDLE_CHANGE_1 = COLOR_CHANGE | NEEDLE_1
NEEDLE_CHANGE_2 = COLOR_CHANGE | NEEDLE_2
NEEDLE_CHANGE_3 = COLOR_CHANGE | NEEDLE_3
NEEDLE_CHANGE_4 = COLOR_CHANGE | NEEDLE_4
...

These should apply both reading and writing. However, simply using & COMMAND_MASK  will convert these more specific commands into simple COLOR_CHANGE commands.

Cython is a Python-like language that gets compiled to C extensions for Python. It's mainly used for speeding up Python code and wrapping C libraries.

For pygit2, Cython would have these advantages:
- python 3 compatibility
- smaller, cleaner code (much less boilerplate; easier to write long docstrings)
- easier maintenance (much DRYer than Python C-API; automatic refcounting)

And some disadvantages:
- it's a complete rewrite
- extra build-time dependency (source releases could come with the compiled .c file, though)
- the language might not be as familiar as C (but it's not that hard to learn)

Do you think it's worth it?

There's a cython branch in my fork; I got the test suite to pass in Pythons 2.5–3.2.

issue_title: Add workaround for the utf8-qp bug in Python 2.6.5
issue_body:
```from email import charset
from email.mime.text import MIMEText

charset.add_charset('utf-8', charset.SHORTEST, charset.QP, 'utf-8')
mt = MIMEText(u'\u00e4', 'plain', 'utf-8')
print bytes(mt)  # Last line should read "=C3=A4, but returns =E4 on Python 2.6.5"```

This makes marrow.mailer unusable on affected Python versions, but Turbomail did not have issues there.
